description.value: This value set includes a smattering of Adjudication Value codes which includes codes to indicate the amounts eligible under the plan, the amount of benefit, copays etc.

copyright.value: This material derives from the HL7 Terminology (THO). THO is copyright ©1989+ Health Level Seven International and is made available under the CC0 designation. For more licensing information see: https://terminology.hl7.org/license

definition.value: The total amount submitted by the provider for the claim, claim group, or line item prior to adjudication.
The fixed amount the member is responsible for paying for a covered service, as defined by the benefit plan.

The maximum amount of the submitted charge that the payer considers as for possible payment as a result of adjudication under the applicable coverage and contractual rules. In some jurisdictions this concept is referred to as the “allowed amount,” “eligible expense,” “payment allowance,” or “negotiated rate.” Some payers may consider the eligible amount and negotiated rate to be equivalent.
The amount for a service or product as determined by terms between the payer and the provider or supplier established outside of a formal network contract.
The portion of the eligible amount that is applied toward the member's deductible obligation and is the member's responsibility.
The portion of the deductible amount that cannot be attributed to a specific claim line or service and is reported at an aggregate level.
The percentage applied to the submitted or eligible amount to determine the portion recognized for adjudication or payment under plan rules.
The amount of applicable tax associated with the service or product, as recognized during adjudication.
The amount payable by the plan for a covered service after application of coverage rules, contractual adjustments, and member cost-sharing requirements.

The amount the insured individual pays, as a set percentage of the cost of covered medical services, as an out-of-pocket payment to the provider or when additional coverage (e.g., secondary coverage is involved). Example: Insured pays 20% and the insurer pays 80%.
The portion of the cost of this service that was deemed not eligible by the insurer because the service or member was not covered by the subscriber's contract.
The amount of the member's obligation for the claim or service.
The amount of the discount for this claim or service applied during adjudication.
The portion of the submitted charge that the payer determined to be subject to in-network contractual pricing and benefit rules for the claim or claim line.
The portion of the submitted charge that the payer determined to be subject to out-of-network pricing and benefit rules for the claim or claim line.

The adjudicated price for the drug product or vaccine administration item before application of manufacturer or other discounts. This amount typically includes one or more pricing components such as ingredient cost, dispensing fee, taxes, and vaccine administration fee.
The total number of units approved or recognized by the payer for the service or benefit under applicable coverage and plan rules.
The number of units applied toward the member’s benefit limits as a result of adjudication of the claim or service.
Indicates the reason a claim or service was denied during adjudication for payment, prior authorization, or predetermination, resulting in no payment or approval for the affected item or service.

Indicates the reason a claim or service request could not be processed due to administrative, technical, or validation issues, and therefore was not adjudicated. This category is used to convey processing failures prior to evaluation or adjudication.
Indicates whether the plan adjudicated the claim as a brand or generic drug.
